judgments.comparative_verdict: Both cover a–c well, but Report 1 answered better overall because it correctly recommends dropping the hardcoded webhook count in favor of pointing at the code, whereas Report 2 merely bumps 4 to 5.

report_1.evidence: New refund provider calls should live in the single vendor-boundary module, `backend/app/billing/gateway.py`... `backend/app/api/invoices.py:14-16` — the charge route builds its own `chargekit.Client(...)`... `backend/app/workers/dunning.py:9-13` — the dunning worker calls `chargekit.Client(...).charge(...)` inline... `backend/app/billing/errors.py:6` imports `from chargekit import errors` — this is the one *sanctioned* exception... so it is not a violation.

report_2.evidence: New provider calls for refunds should live in `backend/app/billing/gateway.py`, as a new accessor (e.g. `refund(...)`)... `backend/app/api/invoices.py:14–16` — `charge_invoice` builds `chargekit.Client(...)`... `backend/app/workers/dunning.py:9–13` — `run_dunning_pass` builds `chargekit.Client(...).charge(...)` inline... `backend/app/billing/errors.py` also imports from `chargekit` (line 6), but it only re-exports error classes and makes no provider calls — this is the explicitly documented exception, not a violation.

report_1.evidence: The actual serializer `InvoiceOut` (`backend/app/models/invoice.py:16-28`) has a sixth field, `external_ref`, and the charge route populates and returns it... The word "exactly" makes this a hard mismatch — the doc is missing `external_ref`.

report_2.evidence: `InvoiceOut` (`backend/app/models/invoice.py:15–28`) now carries a sixth field, `external_ref` (line 22), and `charge_invoice` populates it (`invoices.py:17`)... So the doc undercounts the response shape by one field.

report_1.evidence: drift between this doc and the code is a bug to fix, not a "someday" item, and the fix lands **in the same commit as the code it governs**... the doc edit ships in the **same commit** as the refund code and is called out explicitly in the PR summary

report_2.evidence: these are pre-existing discrepancies independent of refunds, so I'd flag them now (this review) and fix the doc in the same change set that touches this area — ideally *before or alongside* writing refund code... any PR that changes those contracts — including the refund PR — should update `ARCHITECTURE.md` in the same PR.

report_1.evidence: A hard-coded count of handlers is guaranteed to drift (it already has). Rather than just bumping "4"→"5"... I'd drop the count and point at the single source of truth — e.g. "the webhook event types handled are enumerated in `backend/app/api/webhooks.py`."
